title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the photographs and answer the questions.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, and say what benefits outdoor play might have for children's development and what concerns parents might have.

images.label: A
images.description: A group of children playing a boisterous game of football on a grassy park, their faces flushed and muddy with excitement

images.label: B
images.description: Two children building an elaborate den out of branches and leaves in a woodland area, working together with focused expressions

images.label: C
images.description: Children running through a water sprinkler in a garden on a hot summer day, shrieking with laughter and getting completely soaked

followUp.question: Do you think children today have less opportunity for outdoor play than previous generations? Why might that be?

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, and say what the potential benefits and risks of screen time might be for children and how parents might manage it.

images.label: D
images.description: A young child sitting very close to a large TV screen, completely transfixed by a cartoon, in a darkened living room

images.label: E
images.description: A child using an educational tablet app with colourful interactive activities, guided by a parent sitting beside them

images.label: F
images.description: A group of teenagers sitting together but all individually on their phones, barely interacting with each other

followUp.question: Should parents limit their children's screen time strictly, or do you think children can regulate themselves? Why?
